objective: Add 5 features to Stream Manager: !anon commands, rotating panel, tip leaderboard, !anon add/remove, blacklist mode

tech_stack.sandbox_limits: No fetch/XHR/external network. Persistent KV storage. 270x69 panel. 19 event handlers.

constraints: All changes must be mirrored in both handler files AND simulator.js
$limitcam.start(msg, users) is atomic - registered users in allow-list never lose feed
Blacklist subtraction happens in doAnonConverterBlackout() before $limitcam.start()

architecture_snapshot.pattern: Event-driven. shared.js exports utility functions consumed by handler files. simulator.js mirrors all logic for local Node.js testing.

key_modules.shared.js: Utilities, anon converter lifecycle, hidden cam, discount logic, panel cycling
key_modules.handlers/tip-received.js: Tip tracking, per-user tips KV, anon tip tracking
key_modules.handlers/callback.js: Routes callbacks to tipGoalReminder, anonConverterTick, anonConverterRestore, panelCycleTick

conventions: Stream Manager uses 'var' not 'let/const' for IDE compatibility
Each handler file exports one function assigned to onXxx = function(){}
Simulator mirrors handler code exactly, using same function bodies
prior_decisions: Atomic $limitcam.start(msg, users) fix applied (bug: registered users losing access)
Auto-add latecomers in onUserEnter during anon blackout
Official app (streamersuite, v0.0.2) confirmed: 15s/180s defaults match ours
Rotating 3-row panel decided: cycle through views every 6s via $callback

verified_api.$limitcam.start(msg, users?): Atomic - sets message AND allow-list in one call
verified_api.$kv.iter(prefix): Iterate KV keys with prefix filter
